evals.prompt: Assess GEO readiness for the one supplied public page: https://harbor.example/pricing. Capture timestamp: 2026-07-22T09:00:00Z. Raw HTML has title 'Harbor pricing', a pricing table, and no product claims beyond 'Teams save time'. Rendered DOM has the same title and table plus a client-rendered claim 'Harbor cuts onboarding time by 42%'. No source URL, study, customer evidence, analytics, CMS, or model-query capture is supplied. Start public-first and report the current primary source or unavailable for every material conclusion.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode, limits the review to the supplied page, and separates the verified raw/rendered observations from the unsupported rendered statistic. It records missing evidence as unavailable instead of inventing readiness, performance, or model visibility.
assertions: Reports the raw title as verified.
Reports the rendered title as verified.
Reports the rendered 42% claim as verified content.
Does not treat the 42% claim as substantiated.
Identifies source status for each material conclusion.
Marks an absent primary source unavailable.

evals.prompt: Run a site GEO audit for https://atlas.example. The supplied public crawl capture dated 2026-07-22 has 1,200 URLs. Use this deterministic representative sample only: /, /product, /pricing, /docs/getting-started, /blog/ai-search, /integrations/crm, /customers/river, /legal/privacy. The sample contains eight fetched pages; /customers/river returns 503. No logs, sitemap inventory, analytics, Search Console, or complete crawl is available. Do not inspect unlisted URLs or generalize the sample as site-wide fact.
evals.expected_output: Selects site mode and reports a bounded eight-URL representative sample, including the captured 503 response for /customers/river. It keeps response details without supplied captures unavailable and keeps site-wide conclusions inferred or unavailable without complete coverage evidence.
assertions: States that the sample is bounded.
Does not generalize sampled evidence as site-wide verified truth.
Requests broader coverage evidence for site-wide conclusions.
Identifies source status for each site conclusion.
Marks an absent primary source unavailable.

evals.prompt:
Perform a focused GEO rendering audit of https://relay.example/security. At 2026-07-22T09:15:00Z, the raw HTTP HTML contains

Security

and 'SOC 2 report available on request.' The rendered browser DOM contains that content plus 'Trusted by 8,000 security teams' and a citation link whose href is '#'. Browser console has no error. No model crawl, cached copy, source study, or customer list is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused mode for raw-versus-rendered delivery. It reports the DOM mismatch as verified, treats crawler exposure as unavailable or inferred without a relevant primary source, and identifies the non-resolving citation as unsupported rather than proof that the statistic is false.
assertions: Scopes the audit to raw-versus-rendered content delivery.
Reports the raw security heading as verified.
Reports the rendered 8,000-team claim as verified content.
Reports that the 8,000-team claim is absent from raw HTML.
Reports the href '#' citation link as verified.
Labels source support for the 8,000-team claim unavailable.
Does not state that a model crawler saw the rendered claim.
Does not label unobserved crawler behavior verified.
Does not infer crawler behavior from a browser console without errors.
Identifies source status for each rendering conclusion.
Marks an absent primary source unavailable.

evals.prompt: Audit claim provenance on https://lumen.example/research. Capture date: 2026-07-22. Page claim A: '91% of finance leaders recover ROI in 30 days' links to https://lumen.example/research/roi-study-2021.pdf, dated 2021-05-01, which says 19 of 40 survey respondents reported payback within 30 days. Page claim B: 'Most trusted AI platform' has no citation. Page claim C: '2x faster close' links to a customer quote dated 2026-06-15 that says 'our monthly close felt faster' with no measured baseline. No other evidence is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode and traces each claim to its supplied source. It marks the dated PDF as evidence that does not support the 91% wording, marks the uncited superlative unsupported, and avoids converting a qualitative customer quote into a quantified 2x result.
assertions: Reports that 19 of 40 respondents is the supplied PDF result.
Labels the 91% claim unsupported by the supplied PDF.
Labels the 2x claim unsupported by the qualitative quote.
Does not convert 'felt faster' into a 2x measurement.
Does not call the 2021 PDF current evidence for a 2026 claim.
Links each claim conclusion to its supplied source.
Marks an absent primary source unavailable.

evals.prompt: Review entity consistency for owned entity 'Northstar Cloud, Inc.' Public owned evidence captured 2026-07-22: https://northstar.example/about names 'Northstar Cloud, Inc.' and support phone +1-555-0100. Observed third-party evidence: https://directory.example/northstar names 'Northstar AI' and phone +1-555-0199, captured 2026-07-21. The claimed Crunchbase profile URL returns 403 at 2026-07-22T10:00:00Z. No account access exists. Compare only actually observed third-party records with owned records.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused entity-consistency mode. It distinguishes owned facts from the observed directory mismatch and marks the inaccessible claimed Crunchbase record unavailable rather than asserting a mismatch or a correction there.
assertions: Scopes the audit to entity consistency.
Reports the owned legal name as verified.
Reports the owned phone as verified.
Reports the observed directory name as verified.
Reports the observed directory phone as verified.
Reports the observed directory name mismatch.
Reports the observed directory phone mismatch.
Does not assert Crunchbase content without observing it.
Separates owned evidence from third-party evidence.
Names the dated source for every observed entity value.

evals.prompt: Measure observed answer visibility for brand 'Harbor' using this repeated multi-platform panel. Panel A: platform=ChatGPT, product=ChatGPT, model=gpt-4.1, exposure=unexposed; search_mode=web-search; query='best payroll software for 50-person companies'; market=US; language=en; login_state=logged_out; timestamps=2026-07-20T12:00:00Z, 2026-07-21T12:00:00Z, 2026-07-22T12:00:00Z; repetitions=3. Answer 1 mentions Harbor and cites https://harbor.example/pricing plus https://review.example/payroll. Answer 2 does not mention Harbor and cites https://review.example/payroll. Answer 3 returns error 'rate_limited' and has no answer. Panel B: platform=Gemini, product=Gemini, model=unexposed, exposure=unexposed; search_mode=search; same query, market, language, and login_state; timestamps=2026-07-20T12:05:00Z, 2026-07-21T12:05:00Z, 2026-07-22T12:05:00Z; repetitions=3. Each Panel B attempt returns error 'login_required' and no answer. Treat the first URL as an owned citation and the review URL as third-party. Report observed counts and denominators only.
evals.expected_output: Selects visibility mode and records every platform, product, model-or-unexposed, search, query, locale, login, timestamp, repetition, answer, mention, owned citation, third-party citation, and error field. For Panel A it reports answer availability 2/3, brand mentions 1/3 total attempts and 1/2 answered attempts, owned citations 1/3, third-party citations 2/3, and errors 1/3. For Panel B it reports the product unavailable under the logged-out state, zero answered attempts in three, and three login_required errors in three, without asserting a stable rank or share.
assertions: Reports one brand mention in three attempts.
Reports one brand mention in two answered attempts.
Reports one owned citation in three attempts.
Reports two third-party citations in three attempts.
Reports one error in three attempts.
Reports Gemini as unavailable under the logged-out state.
Reports zero Gemini answers in three attempts.
Reports three Gemini login_required errors in three attempts.
Does not treat Gemini unavailability as a zero mention rate.
Separates observed visibility from readiness scoring.
Does not infer a stable rank.
Does not infer a market share.

evals.prompt: Run a competitive visibility comparison for the modifier 'best payroll software for 50-person companies'. Same panel controls: platform=Perplexity, product=Perplexity, model=unexposed, search_mode=search, market=US, language=en, login_state=logged_out, timestamp=2026-07-22T12:30:00Z. Repetition 1 answer names Harbor and Ledgerly; cites https://harbor.example/pricing and https://ledgerly.example. Repetition 2 answer names Ledgerly only; cites https://ledgerly.example. Use competitor comparison as a modifier, not a separate primary mode.
evals.expected_output: Selects visibility mode with a competitive modifier. It compares observed mentions and citations for the named entities on the same controlled panel, reports Harbor mentions 1/2 and Ledgerly mentions 2/2, and does not convert this two-run sample into a stable rank.
assertions: Uses the same controls for both entities.
Does not call Ledgerly rank one.
Does not infer an enduring competitive advantage.

evals.prompt: Investigate stochastic answer variation for a fixed visibility panel: platform=Claude, product=Claude, model=unexposed, search_mode=no-search, query='What is Harbor payroll?', market=US, language=en, login_state=logged_out, timestamp=2026-07-22T13:00:00Z. Five repetitions produce: 1 Harbor named; 2 no brand named; 3 Harbor named; 4 no brand named; 5 Harbor named. No citations are present in any answer. No earlier or later panel exists.
evals.expected_output: Selects visibility mode and reports three mentions in five repetitions, two non-mentions in five, and zero citations in five. It treats variation as an observed panel result and declines to assert a decline, causal explanation, or stable visibility rate beyond the stated sample.
assertions: Reports three Harbor mentions in five repetitions.
Reports two non-mentions in five repetitions.
Reports zero citations in five repetitions.
Does not claim a decline without a comparison panel.
Does not infer a causal explanation for variation.
Does not claim a stable visibility rate beyond five repetitions.

evals.prompt: Investigate a citation-and-mention decline incident. Matched before artifact dated 2026-06-01: platform=ChatGPT, product=ChatGPT, model=gpt-4.1, exposure=unexposed, search_mode=web-search, query='best payroll software for 50-person companies', market=US, language=en, login_state=logged_out, repetitions=4; Harbor mentions=4, Harbor owned citations=3, answers=4, errors=0. Matched after artifact dated 2026-07-01 has identical controls and repetitions=4; Harbor mentions=1, Harbor owned citations=0, answers=4, errors=0. No deployment log, provider changelog, traffic data, or crawl data is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects incident mode and uses the dated matched artifacts as the comparison boundary. It reports observed mentions changing from 4/4 to 1/4 and owned citations from 3/4 to 0/4, then keeps cause unavailable or inferred without deployment, provider, traffic, or crawl evidence.
assertions: Uses 2026-06-01 as the before artifact date.
Uses 2026-07-01 as the after artifact date.
Does not state an incident cause as verified.
Keeps observed visibility counts separate from readiness scoring.
Does not infer a stable rank.

evals.prompt: The VP says, 'Harbor disappeared from AI answers last quarter.' No platform, product, model, exposure, search mode, query, market, language, login state, timestamp, answer capture, citation capture, repetition count, or before/after artifact is supplied. Do not manufacture a baseline.
evals.expected_output: Selects incident mode because a decline is reported, but labels the decline reported rather than verified. It asks for a matched panel and dated answer artifacts before calculating any change or diagnosing a cause.
assertions: Labels the reported decline as reported.
Does not label the reported decline verified.
Does not calculate a decline rate.

evals.prompt: Focused GEO question for https://cobalt.example: robots.txt captured 2026-07-22 says 'User-agent: * Disallow: /private/' and has no directives naming an AI provider. The site has no published training policy. A public search result for /docs exists dated 2026-07-21. There is no provider fetch log, no model training disclosure, and no user-triggered browser session. Explain what can and cannot be concluded about search indexing, model training, search-browse retrieval, and a user-triggered fetch.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused mode and distinguishes robots directives from each requested mechanism. It treats the public result as evidence of search indexing for /docs, treats training and provider fetch behavior as unavailable, and does not conflate user-triggered fetches with automated search-browse retrieval.
assertions: Reports the absence of named AI-provider directives.
Reports the dated public search result for /docs.
Treats the public result as evidence of search indexing for /docs.
Does not treat the public result as proof of model training.
Distinguishes search indexing from model training.
Distinguishes search-browse retrieval from a user-triggered fetch.
Does not generalize robots directives to every crawler behavior.

evals.prompt: Audit optional AI-discovery artifacts for https://ember.example. Public capture dated 2026-07-22: /llms.txt returns 200 with 'Ember is a workflow tool'; /okf.json returns 200 with invalid JSON '{name: Ember}'; /knowledge-bundle.zip returns 200 and contains one README dated 2024-01-01 saying 'draft'. No platform documentation requires these files. No benchmark, crawl log, answer capture, or current primary source validating the formats is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Selects focused mode and treats llms.txt, OKF, and a knowledge bundle as experimental optional artifacts. It reports their observed delivery states, records invalid JSON and stale bundle content, and makes no mandatory-schema, benchmark, crawl, or visibility claim from their existence.
assertions: Scopes the audit to experimental discovery artifacts.
Reports the bundle README date as 2024-01-01.
Requests review of stale bundle content.
Treats the knowledge bundle as optional.
Does not require an AI file.
Does not require an AI schema.
Does not deduct a benchmark score for an absent optional artifact.
Does not deduct a benchmark score for an invalid optional artifact.
Does not claim a crawler uses any artifact.
Does not claim an artifact improves visibility.
Marks an absent primary format source unavailable.

evals.prompt: Assess this attributable private monitoring export. Export owner='Harbor Growth Ops'; generated_at=2026-07-22T08:00:00Z; run_id='mon-884'; platform=ChatGPT; product=ChatGPT; model=gpt-4.1; exposure=unexposed; search_mode=web-search; query='best payroll software for 50-person companies'; market=US; language=en; login_state=logged_out; repetitions=10; answered=9; errors=1; Harbor_mentions=4; Harbor_owned_citations=3; third_party_citations=7. Raw answer records are absent. No public capture exists.
evals.expected_output: Selects visibility mode and accepts the export as attributable private evidence with stated limits. It reports the supplied counts with their denominators, labels the absent raw answers unavailable for answer-level verification, and neither treats private monitoring as public evidence nor derives rank, traffic, or conversion.
assertions: Reports nine answered attempts in ten repetitions.
Reports one error in ten repetitions.
Reports four Harbor mentions in ten repetitions.
Reports three Harbor owned citations in ten repetitions.
Reports seven third-party citations in ten repetitions.
Labels the export attributable private evidence.
Does not label the export public evidence.

evals.prompt: Audit https://harbor.example for 'payroll software'. The request asks why Google organic rankings fell from position 4 to 17 and asks for backlink remediation. No AI answer, answer panel, citation, model, or assistant-product evidence is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Routes this traditional search-ranking and backlink request out of GEO audit scope to the SEO audit capability. It does not invent GEO visibility findings or promise ranking recovery.
assertions: Routes the request to SEO audit.
Identifies Google organic rank analysis as traditional SEO work.
Identifies backlink remediation as traditional SEO work.
Does not run a GEO visibility panel.

evals.prompt: Implement server-side rendering, create llms.txt, write JSON-LD, and deploy the changes to https://harbor.example. This is an implementation request; no audit evidence is supplied.
evals.expected_output: Routes the implementation-only request out of scope to the appropriate engineering or implementation capability. It can identify required validation evidence but does not present an audit, a mandatory llms.txt requirement, or untested implementation output as verified.
assertions: Routes server-side rendering to implementation work.
Does not state that llms.txt is mandatory.
Does not present JSON-LD as verified without validation.

evals.prompt: Produce a standalone HTML GEO audit report that opens locally. Include only these inline artifacts: a CSS text file 'body { color: red; }'; an SVG image with <script>alert(1)</script>; an HTML-disguised file named 'report.png' whose bytes begin '<html>'; an animated GIF; a 12 MB PNG; and aggregate metrics 'mentions=17' with no panel records, denominator, platform, query, timestamp, or source. The requested report has no other evidence.
evals.expected_output: Selects out-of-scope routing for report production without audit-grade evidence, while treating standalone HTML as an output format rather than a substitute for audit semantics. It rejects or safely excludes unsafe, disguised, animated, oversized, and un-attributable aggregate assets; it does not turn the aggregate number into a visibility metric.
assertions: Treats standalone HTML as an output format.
Does not treat HTML formatting as audit evidence.
Excludes the CSS text asset from report output.
Excludes the scripted SVG asset from report output.
Excludes the HTML-disguised PNG asset from report output.
Excludes the animated GIF asset from report output.
Excludes the 12 MB PNG asset from report output.
Labels the aggregate mention count unavailable for visibility measurement.
Does not report a visibility rate from mentions=17.
Does not embed executable SVG content.

evals.prompt: Score this one-page GEO readiness fixture using only the fixed card: Access 20, Extractability 25, Evidence 25, Entity 20, Measurement 10. Checks: ACC-01 pass 20/20; EXT-01 partial 12.5/25; EVD-01 unavailable 0/25; ENT-01 fail 0/20; MEA-01 not_applicable 0/10. Findings: harbor-EXT-01-01 supports EXT-01; harbor-ENT-01-01 supports ENT-01. Required formulas: evidence coverage = assessed applicable maximum / all applicable maximum * 100; readiness score = assessed earned / assessed applicable maximum * 100. Never award or deduct points solely because evidence is unavailable or not applicable. Baseline status is RED because no production GEO contract exists.
evals.expected_output: Selects page mode and applies the fixed readiness card exactly. Applicable maximum is 90 because MEA-01 is not applicable; assessed applicable maximum is 65 because EVD-01 is unavailable; assessed earned is 32.5; evidence coverage is 65/90*100 = 72.22%; readiness score is 32.5/65*100 = 50.00%. It shows RED because the baseline has no production GEO contract and links the scored deductions to unique findings.
assertions: Uses evidence coverage = assessed applicable maximum / all applicable maximum * 100.
Uses readiness score = assessed earned / assessed applicable maximum * 100.
Does not award points for EVD-01.
Does not deduct points solely because EVD-01 is unavailable.
Excludes MEA-01 from the applicable denominator.
Does not reuse a finding ID for another deduction.
Shows RED because baseline has no production GEO contract.
Does not infer observed answer visibility from readiness score.

evals.prompt: Run a focused, competitive GEO review of https://harbor.example/pricing against https://ledgerly.example/pricing. Supplied public capture timestamp: 2026-07-22T15:00:00Z. Harbor raw HTML says 'Payroll for teams' and rendered DOM says 'Payroll for teams trusted by 3,000 companies' with no citation. Ledgerly raw and rendered HTML both say 'Payroll automation for finance teams' and link to https://ledgerly.example/customers, which returns 404. evals.expected_output: Selects page mode with focused and competitive modifiers. It compares only the two supplied pages, reports the observed delivery and source defects, marks answer visibility and third-party validation unavailable, and avoids benchmark deductions, crawler generalization, fabricated metrics, uplift, or rank claims.
assertions: Limits comparison to the two supplied pricing pages.
Reports Harbor's rendered 3,000-company claim as verified content.
Labels support for Harbor's 3,000-company claim unavailable.
Reports Ledgerly text in raw HTML.
Reports Ledgerly text in rendered HTML.
Does not apply a benchmark deduction.
Does not generalize browser evidence to crawler behavior.
Identifies source status for each comparison conclusion.
Marks an absent primary source unavailable.
